summary: defi-threat-scanner exfiltrates host info and environment variables to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json declares a lifecycle script (line 32) that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers hostname/cwd/env data, and POSTs it to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js compounds the behavior: it imports fs, os, https, and child_process, calls execSync (line 233, including 'whoami'), reads os.hostname() and os.platform() (lines 234, 246), enumerates filesystem paths via fs.existsSync (lines 93, 114, 217), reads process.env (line 222), and POSTs/GETs the collected data to https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 142-143, 161). The destination is a personal GitHub Pages site unrelated to any legitimate publisher, with no version pinning, no hash verification, and no documented purpose for receiving installer host data. The package name advertises a 'DeFi threat scanner' but the actual behavior is host reconnaissance and exfiltration of environment variables and system identity to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
